rulings.Case Title: PETER REDKO DPM VS. PODIATRIC MEDICINE BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees Petitioner Peter Redko, Dpm'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To Government Code Section 800
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2018, Line 11, Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees Petitioner Peter Redko, Dpm'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To Government Code Section 800. Petitioner Peter Redko's motion for attorney fees and costs is DENIED. Government Code §800 allows a minimal attorney fee award (maximum: $7,500) if a public entity acted in a "wholly arbitrary or capricious" manner in an administrative proceeding. (Stirling v. Agricultural Labor Relations Bd. (1987) 189 Cal.App.3d 1305, 1312.) Attorney fees may not be awarded if the entity's action was "clearly erroneous;" rather, the action must be "not supported by a fair or substantial reason, a stubborn insistence on following unauthorized conduct, or a bad faith legal dispute." (Id.) I find that respondent's actions here - misreading its authority under state law - were the former, not the latter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN-YVES LENDORMY VS. KYLE HANSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JEAN-YVES LENDORMY's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment. Plaintiff Jean-Yves Lendormy's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Murphy and Hanson's failure to respond to the petition to confirm within 10 days after service jurisdictionally bars Murphy and Hanson from now seeking to vacate the award. (See CCP §§ 1290 and 1290.6; Oaktree Capital Management, LP v. Bernard (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 60, 66; Eternity Investments, Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal.App. 4th 739, 742; Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1210-1212, (cases stating CCP § 473 relief is available are inconsistent with holding in Maynard v. Brandon (2005) 36 Cal.4th 364, 372.) CCP § 1290.6 provides that the court may extend the time period based on good cause. The court can no longer "extend" the time period because the 10-day period has already elapsed. In addition, even if CCP § 473 relief were available, counsel McElhenney's declaration fails to set forth such a basis for relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: POLYMATHIC PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL VS. DEAN LUCA KRAUSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Sets One And Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF POLYMATHIC PROPERTIES, INC., MEYER CORPORATION, U.S. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Sets One And Two. On September 12, 2018, the parties stipulated to allow plaintiffs to file a third amended complaint and a second amended answer to the cross-complaint in this action. The motions to permit those amended pleadings are GRANTED. From review of the new pleadings, I note that they appear to be a wholesale rewrite of the prior pleadings. I also note defendant's statement that, while stipulating to filing, he does "not agree that any of the claims asserted in these documents are properly pleaded" or "timely." This strongly suggests that motions on the pleadings are in the offing. I therefore STAY all other pending motions - including all discovery motions and the motion to bifurcate - until the pleadings are settled and the case's scope can be ascertained. Any party may then move to lift the stay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY H. KU VS. ANNA KU WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jerry H. Kus Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 21, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JERRY KU'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JERRY H. KU'S MOTION TO QUASH THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA DIRECTED TO HIS ATTORNEY FOR PRODUCTION OF "YOUR COMPLETE FILE IN CONNECTION WITH JERRY KU, INCLUDING BY NOT LIMITED TO BILLING RECORDS, EMAIL COMMUNICATIONS AND DIGITAL RECORDS"IS GRANTED. ON THE GROUNDS THAT IT IS OVERLY BROAD BOTH AS TO TIME AND SUBJECT MATTER. IN ADDITION, IT CALLS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WHICH ARE SUBJECT TO THE ATTORNEY CLIENT AND PRIVACY PRIVILEGES. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED HIS PRIVILEGES TO THE ENTIRE FILE AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT ARGUES THERE IS A WAIVER TO A SPECIFIC SUBJECT MATTER WHICH MIGHT BE RELEVANT TO THE QUIET TITLE ACTION, DEFENDANTS HAVE MADE NO SHOWING OF A NEED FOR THESE DOCUMENTS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 21, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant U.S. Security Associates, Inc., To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Damages Filed On July 25, 2018
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT U.S. SECURITY ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion Of Defendant U.S. Security Associates, Inc., To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Damages Filed On July 25, 2018. OFF CALENDAR. U.S. Security Associates, Inc. was dismissed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/RBU)
